Gene_Accession_Number.reason: AT3G11340 is listed in Accession numbers, and GenBank accession Q9C768.1 is given in the heterologous expression section.

Source_Species.reason: The study uses Arabidopsis plants throughout; plant materials are all in Arabidopsis thaliana Col-0 background.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Arabidopsis thaliana is explicitly stated in the abstract and Materials and methods.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Arabidopsis thaliana is explicitly the experimental species.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Col-0 is explicitly stated as the wild type/ecotype background.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: UGT76B1 constructs/mutants are in the Col-0 background.

Enzyme_Name.reason: UGT76B1 is described as a uridine diphosphate-dependent glycosyltransferase / glycosyltransferase 76B1.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: UGT76B1 is explicitly a UGT/glycosyltransferase; family 1 UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase is directly inferable from the UGT naming and context.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The paper shows UGT76B1 modifies NHP to NHP-OGlc and also glycosylates SA, fitting NHP metabolism / SA glycosylation pathway.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Results explicitly show NHP O-glycosylation to NHP-OGlc and prior/confirmed SA glycosylation to SAG.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: The paper states UGT76B1 acts downstream of FMO1 and is involved in NHP turnover/homeostasis via glycosylation.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Results show recombinant UGT76B1 forms NHP-OGlc from NHP in vitro and also glycosylates SA and ILA; UDP-glucose was used as donor.

Primary_Substrate.reason: NHP and UDP-glucose are the substrates used in the in vitro reaction; NHP is identified as an in vivo substrate.

Primary_Product.reason: The abstract and results identify 1-O-glucosyl-pipecolic acid / NHP-OGlc as product.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: The study directly ties UGT76B1 to NHP turnover/homeostasis through formation of NHP-OGlc.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper repeatedly describes UGT76B1 as having a central/major role in regulating NHP homeostasis, while not calling it rate-limiting; the wording here preserves that nuance.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The study centers on NHP homeostasis and conversion to NHP-OGlc.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: NHP is described as a plant immune regulator derived from pipecolic acid; class as non-protein amino acid-derived defense metabolite is reasonable from paper context.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The abstract states NHP is a regulator for plant innate immunity and SAR and contributes to resistance phenotypes.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Measurements were made in leaves, including local and systemic leaves in the D9-NHP transport experiments.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: UGT76B1 was identified as co-expressed with FMO1; discussion states induction of UGT76B1 by P.s.m. infection; mutant transcript absence shown in supplemental data.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Pathogen induction of UGT76B1 is stated in the discussion.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: These treatments are explicitly used: Pseudomonas syringae ES4326 infection, UV-C treatment, and exogenous D9-NHP infiltration.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods appear in Results/Methods: T-DNA and CRISPR knockouts, targeted and nontargeted metabolomics, recombinant enzyme assays, kinetics, MS/MS, and fmo1 genetic interaction.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show ugt76b1 mutants lack NHP-OGlc, accumulate more NHP and SA, and display dwarfism plus constitutive resistance.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each numbered statement is directly supported by Results and abstract.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: The paper shows co-expression and genetic pathway relationship with FMO1, but not a physical protein-protein interaction.

Omics_Data.reason: Metabolomics data are central, and MetaboLights accession MTBLS2334 is explicitly provided.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Loss of UGT76B1 causes dwarf phenotype, dark green leaves, constitutive defense, supporting a growth-defense tradeoff.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a forward-looking application inference; the paper discusses balancing growth and defense but does not directly propose editing strategies or advise partial modulation.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper directly shows SA and ILA glycosylation in vitro and concludes UGT76B1 is the only enzyme O-glycosylating NHP in planta under their data; D9-NHP movement without glycosylation is also shown.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: AT1G19250 is explicitly listed in Accession numbers.

Source_Species.reason: FMO1 studied here is from Arabidopsis.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Arabidopsis thaliana is the experimental species throughout.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: All plants are in Col-0 background.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: fmo1-1 and FMO1-3D are in Col-0 background according to plant materials.

Enzyme_Name.reason: FMO1 is explicitly referred to as FLAVIN-DEPENDENT MONOOXYGENASE 1 in the abstract.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The name itself supports flavin-dependent monooxygenase.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The paper states FMO1 is an NHP biosynthetic enzyme.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Results state fmo1 mutants cannot generate NHP from Pip, supporting the Pip-to-NHP branch.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: UGT76B1 is shown to act downstream of FMO1, so FMO1 is upstream in the pathway.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper states fmo1-1 cannot generate NHP from Pip and calls FMO1 an NHP biosynthetic enzyme.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Pip is explicitly stated as the precursor converted by FMO1 to NHP.

Primary_Product.reason: NHP is explicitly the product missing in fmo1 mutants.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: FMO1 directly forms the target product NHP according to the paper.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The study treats FMO1 as essential/key for NHP formation, though not formally rate-limiting; the wording says key biosynthetic enzyme, which is supported.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: NHP is the metabolite controlled by FMO1 in this paper.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: NHP is a defense metabolite derived from pipecolic acid; this classification is consistent with the paper.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The abstract describes NHP as regulator of innate immunity and SAR.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: NHP measurements were performed in leaves.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Results show FMO1 transcript levels are significantly reduced in ugt76b1-1 after infection, and FMO1-3D shows strong resistance phenotype.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: FMO1-related phenotypes/metabolites are examined after P.s.m. infection and UV-C treatment.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Results use fmo1-1 loss-of-function, FMO1-3D gain-of-function, double mutants with ugt76b1, and metabolite profiling.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: fmo1 plants do not accumulate NHP or NHP-OGlc, and fmo1 suppresses the dwarf and enhanced resistance phenotypes of ugt76b1.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summarized findings are directly supported by Results.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: UGT76B1 is genetically related in pathway logic, but no physical protein interaction is shown.

Omics_Data.reason: Metabolomics are used and the study accession MTBLS2334 is provided.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Loss increasing susceptibility is supported, but explicit gain-of-function-associated dwarfism/autoimmunity for FMO1 in this paper is only indirectly linked via prior reports and comparison to FMO1-3D resistance; not all details are directly shown here.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is an application-oriented inference not explicitly discussed as a breeding strategy in the paper.

Other_Important_Info.reason: FMO1 is indeed central to the genetic logic but serves as comparator/background rather than the newly characterized enzyme of the study.
